name: Settings Full
description: Complete account settings with profile, security (password, MFA, sessions), preferences (theme, notifications, language), and danger zone.

page_briefs.profile: Profile settings form with avatar upload, display name, email, and bio fields. Save button at the bottom of each section. Clean grouped form layout.
page_briefs.security: Security settings with password change form (current + new password fields), MFA toggle with setup flow, and active sessions table showing device, location, and last-active with revoke buttons.
page_briefs.preferences: Preferences form with theme selector (light/dark/auto toggle), notification preference checkboxes, language dropdown, and timezone selector.
page_briefs.danger: Danger zone section with red-bordered cards for destructive actions. Export data button, deactivate account toggle, and delete account button with confirmation dialog warning.

directives: Settings pages use the blueprint's primary shell (`sidebar-main` or `top-nav-main` depending on blueprint). Sidebar nav shows settings sub-pages as `d-nav-link` entries.
Forms use `d-surface` cards with `d-control` inputs. Each settings section is a distinct `d-surface` — do NOT nest one d-surface inside another.

Dangerous actions (delete account, revoke all sessions) use `d-interactive[data-variant="danger"]` + `d-annotation[data-status="warning"]` for the warning text. A confirmation dialog uses `d-modal` + `d-modal-panel[data-size="sm"]`.

Theme toggle is a compact `d-icon-btn[data-size="sm"]` in the shell header AND a labeled control in settings/preferences. State flows through the document root `data-mode` attribute, not through inline styles. Persist user choice to `localStorage.setItem('decantr_theme_mode', mode)` and hydrate via `document.documentElement.setAttribute('data-mode', localStorage.getItem('decantr_theme_mode') ?? 'dark')` BEFORE React mounts (place in index.html or a sync top-level script) — avoids flash-of-wrong-theme.
MFA toggle rendering: use `d-step-chip[data-step-state="done|pending"]` for completed/pending MFA methods. Session list rows use `d-data` table family.

Breadcrumbs (when present in the shell header) derive from the current route + the scaffold-pack route table — do NOT hand-map breadcrumbs in each settings page. Use a small helper that reads `window.location.pathname`, splits on `/`, and looks up labels from the navigation_items list. This keeps breadcrumbs in sync when routes change.

shells.inherit: Inherits shell from parent archetype or app context
